channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Congress considers expanded US–Israel defence technology cooperation under NDAA proposal
item.description: A new provision included in the US House of Representatives’ version of the National Defence Authorisation Act (NDAA) for fiscal year 2027 is being discussed for significantly expanding military and t...

item.title: Canada establishes independent board to review Afghan veterans for the Victoria Cross
item.description: Canada has announced the creation of an Independent Military Honours Review Board to examine cases of Afghan War veterans for potential awards of the Canadian Victoria Cross. The move follows decades ...

item.title: Governor Otti, Orji Kalu, others mourn death of AIG Kanayo Uzuegbu
item.description: The Assistant Inspector-General of Police in charge of Zone 9, Kanayo Uzuegbu, has passed away following cancer-related complications. His death was confirmed after Abia State Governor Alex Otti annou...

item.title: Rod Stewart postpones two Las Vegas shows to recover from sinus infection
item.description: Sir Rod Stewart, the 81-year-old British rock legend, was compelled to cancel two of his Las Vegas residency shows at The Colosseum at Caesars Palace, originally scheduled for last Friday and Saturday...

item.title: US judge reviews controversial settlement in Donald Trump’s US$10 billion IRS lawsuit
item.description: A US federal judge has reopened scrutiny of a controversial settlement involving Donald Trump’s US$10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), raising questions about potential conf...

item.title: Vietnam President To Lam’s planned Senate visit in Manila canceled
item.description: The scheduled visit of Vietnamese President and Communist Party General Secretary To Lam to the Philippine Senate on Monday, June 1, was canceled, according to a report from the Senate’s Office of Int...

item.title: Fremantle strengthen AFL premiership claims after commanding win over Brisbane
item.description: Fremantle have further enhanced their reputation as genuine AFL contenders following a convincing away victory over Brisbane at the Gabba, a venue traditionally considered difficult for visiting sides...

item.title: Trump claims Iran has agreed to halt nuclear weapons development amid ongoing negotiations
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has stated that Iran has agreed not to pursue nuclear weapons development, describing it as a key guarantee secured during ongoing diplomatic efforts between Washington and...

item.title: Kenya reassures public after 16 suspected Ebola cases test negative nationwide
item.description: The Government of Kenya has confirmed that all 16 suspected Ebola Virus Disease (EVD) cases identified across the country have tested negative, easing public concern amid heightened regional alertness...

item.title: Indian Schengen visa applications rise in 2025 despite higher costs and rejections
item.description: In 2025, Indians continued to rank as the third largest group of applicants for Schengen visas globally, according to data released by the European Commission. Around 11.5 lakh applications were submi...

item.title: Questions raised after Dutch police incident involving pregnant woman at asylum centre in Zeist
item.description: A strong public reaction has emerged in the Netherlands after a video surfaced online showing police officers allegedly using excessive force against a pregnant woman at an asylum seekers’ centre in Z...

item.title: Curfew imposed as protests continue outside Newark ICE detention facility following weekend clashes
item.description: Protests continued outside the Delaney Hall Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detention facility in Newark, New Jersey, following a weekend marked by clashes between demonstrators and law enfo...

item.title: HIV Prevention in South Africa: Addressing the Gap Among Mobile Male Workers
item.description: South Africa recently received its first shipment of Lenacapavir, a long-acting injectable that can prevent HIV with just two shots per year, showing nearly 100% effectiveness in clinical trials. The ...

item.title: Simba SC step back from Tanzanian Premier League title race amid season challenges
item.description: Tanzanian football giants Simba SC have officially announced that they are withdrawing from the Premier League championship race for the current season, a move that has shocked many fans and football ...

item.title: Majority of Filipinos Support Senate Impeachment Trial for VP Sara Duterte, Survey Shows
item.description: A recent survey by OCTA Research indicates that support for a Senate impeachment trial against Vice President Sara Duterte has risen to 74% as of April 2026, up from 69% in March. The Tugon ng Masa (T...

item.title: Jinggoy Estrada files motions questioning plunder and graft charges
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada has filed omnibus motions challenging the plunder and graft charges against him, according to a report by ABS-CBN News. The motions reportedly question the legal basis and vali...

item.title: Rafael Jodar faces renewed criticism over on-court behaviour at the French Open
item.description: Spanish tennis player Rafael Jodar has attracted further criticism during the French Open following two separate incidents involving young event assistants. The 19-year-old, seeded 27th in the tournam...

item.title: Philippines and Vietnam strengthen strategic partnership in high-level talks in Malacañang
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. welcomed Vietnamese President and Communist Party General Secretary Tô Lâm to Malacañang Palace on Monday morning for high-level bilateral talks aimed at strengthening t...

item.title: Springbok fly-half Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu sidelined by serious ankle injury
item.description: In a URC quarter-final in Cape Town, Stormers fly-half Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu limped off after scoring a try in a 44-21 win against Cardiff. Stormers coach John Dobson indicated the injury appears ...

item.title: Experts discuss concerns about ‘AI psychosis’ and shifting attitudes toward AI adoption
item.description: This TechCrunch discussion explores the growing debate around so-called “AI psychosis,” a term sparked by Box founder Aaron Levie’s comments suggesting that some tech CEOs may be overly immersed or di...
